headline: American Express reported March 2026 credit metrics, highlighting stable delinquency rates alongside a modest sequential increase in net write-offs for U.S. Consumer and Small Business loans.
investor_impact: Stable 30-day past due rates indicate that early-stage credit stress remains contained among AXP's premium customer base. However, the sequential creep in net write-off rates (Consumer reaching 2.2% and Small Business reaching 2.9%) will keep investors focused on credit normalization trends and potential impacts on provision expenses in upcoming earnings.
key_figures: U.S. Consumer 30 days past due rate: 1.4% (flat Jan-Mar 2026)
U.S. Consumer net write-off rate: 2.2% in March (up from 2.0% in Feb)
U.S. Small Business total loans: $32.2 billion (March 2026)
U.S. Small Business 30 days past due rate: 1.7% (flat Jan-Mar 2026)
U.S. Small Business net write-off rate: 2.9% in March (up from 2.8% in Feb)
Total Card Member loans held for investment: $129.7 billion
Lending Trust annualized default rate: 1.2% in March (down from 1.3% in Feb)
